scenarios.title: displays the personalised welcome banner on the dashboard @positive
scenarios.userAction: Load the dashboard as an authenticated user with the A/B welcome-banner variant forced ON via fixture cookie, and verify the banner is visible and contains the user's display name.
expectedAssertions: welcome banner is visible after dashboard load
welcome banner contains the authenticated user's display name (or matches EXPECTED_GREETING_PATTERN)

scenarios.title: shows at least one item in the notifications widget @positive
scenarios.userAction: Load the dashboard as an authenticated user, interact with the notifications widget (expand or open if needed), and verify at least one notification item is visible with content.
expectedAssertions: notifications widget is visible after dashboard load
first notification item is visible after opening the widget
first notification item contains notification text

locatorTable.notes: Assumes login form has <label> for the email input. Moves to authenticated.fixture.ts. Fall back: getByRole('textbox', { name: /email/i }).
Assumes login form has <label> for the password input. Moves to authenticated.fixture.ts. Fall back: getByRole('textbox', { name: /password/i }).
Already role-based; widen to regex for copy-variation resilience. Moves to authenticated.fixture.ts.
No DOM snapshot. Element could be <section>, <aside>, <div>, role=alert, or role=status. See pin 1.
No DOM snapshot. Same uncertainty as welcome-banner. See pin 2.
No DOM snapshot. Items could be <li>, <tr>, <article>, or a custom element. See pin 3.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q1 unresolved: .welcome-banner element role unknown — assumed sectioning element with accessible name matching /welcome/.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Ask FE team for the element type. If no region/accessible-name, add data-testid='welcome-banner' and switch to page.getByTestId('welcome-banner').

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q2 unresolved: .notifications-widget element role unknown — assumed sectioning element with accessible name matching /notifications/.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Ask FE team for the element type. If no region/accessible-name, add data-testid='notifications-widget' and switch to page.getByTestId('notifications-widget').

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q3 unresolved: .notification-item element type unknown — assumed <li> inside <ul>/<ol>.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Confirm element is <li>. If not (e.g. <tr>, <article>), add data-testid='notification-item' and switch to page.getByTestId('notification-item').first().

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q1-auth: assumes login form has a <label> associated with the email input.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: If no label exists, use page.getByRole('textbox', { name: /email/i }) or page.getByPlaceholder(/email/i) as fallback.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q1-auth: assumes login form has a <label> associated with the password input.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: If no label exists, use page.getByRole('textbox', { name: /password/i }) or page.getByPlaceholder(/password/i) as fallback.
